task_completion.description: Load the CT Cranium DICOM scan into InVesalius 3
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 application is open and displays the loaded CT Cranium scan, typically showing 2D axial, coronal, and sagittal slices of a head.

task_completion.description: Select the Bone preset for segmentation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The threshold or segmentation panel shows the 'Bone' preset selected, or explicitly displays the Hounsfield Unit (HU) range of 226 to 3071.

task_completion.description: Create a 3D surface reconstruction of the skull
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer pane in InVesalius displays a fully reconstructed 3D model of the skull based on the bone segmentation.

task_completion.description: Configure the 3D viewer with a solid white background
task_completion.visual_evidence: The background of the 3D viewer pane behind the skull is solid white, with no dark colors or gradients.

task_completion.description: Orient the camera to a standard anterior (frontal) view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D skull model is oriented so that it is facing directly forward toward the camera (eye sockets and nasal cavity visible head-on).

task_completion.description: Export the rendered image as a PNG to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog box shows the file being saved as 'skull_frontal_pub.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory, or a file manager/terminal confirms the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the InVesalius 3 graphical interface (clicking menus, adjusting thresholds, rotating the 3D view) rather than using terminal scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The 3D render was genuinely generated within the software
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D skull is visible inside the actual InVesalius 3 workspace with its surrounding UI elements and orientation widgets, not just an external image opened in an image viewer.

integrity.description: The image was exported using the software's export functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: The export process is visible via InVesalius dialogs, proving the agent did not simply download a pre-made image from the internet to the target directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using InVesalius 3. The correct threshold range for the Bone preset is 226-3071 HU. The background must be solid white (RGB > 230). The final output must be saved as a PNG to '/home/ga/Documents/skull_frontal_pub.png'. The dataset is a sample CT Cranium scan.
